title: Bridge Essay: The functional role of conscious sensation of movement.
concept: Free will — sense of agency, voluntary action, intentional binding, volitional control.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nested Agency** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Free will — sense of agency, voluntary action, intentional binding, volitional control.

**The functional role of conscious sensation of movement.** - Neurosci Biobehav Rev (2024). DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2024.105813. PMID: 39019245. This paper proposes a new framework for investigating neural signals sufficient for a conscious sensation of movement and their role in motor control. We focus on signals sufficient for proprioceptive awareness, particularly from muscle spindle activation and from primary motor cortex (M1). Our review of muscle vibration studies reveals that afferent signals alone can induce conscious sensations of movement.
